IDirectSound8::SynchPlayback

Synchronizes the playback of multiple DirectSound buffers and/or streams.

HRESULT SynchPlayback();

Parameters

None.

Return Values

Returns DS_OK if successful; returns an error code otherwise.

Remarks

SynchPlayback causes all buffers and/or streams placed in the SYNCHPLAYBACK state to begin playback simultaneously in the same sample frame. This allows for sample accurate playback of multiple voices. Buffers can be placed in this state by calling IDirectSoundBuffer:Play/IDirectSoundBuffer8::PlayEx with the DSBPLAY_SYNCHPLAYBACK flag, or by calling IDirectSoundBuffer8::Pause/IDirectSoundBuffer8::PauseEx with the DSBPAUSE_SYNCHPLAYBACK flag. Similarly, streams can be placed in this state by calling IDirectSoundStream::Pause/IDirectSoundStream::PauseEx with the DSSTREAMPAUSE_SYNCHPLAYBACK flag.

A maximum of 29 hardware voices can be placed in the SYNCHPLAYBACK state.

Voices in the SYNCHPLAYBACK state are normally paused until SynchPlayback is called. Querying the status on a buffer or stream in the SYNCHPLAYBACK state will return PAUSED. When SynchPlayback is called, all voices in this state are resumed and are guaranteed to start in the same sample frame: 

// Get first buffer ready to play
hr = pBuffer1->Play(0, 0, DSBPLAY_LOOPING | DSBPLAY_SYNCHPLAYBACK);
// Get second buffer ready to play
hr = pBuffer2->Play(0, 0, DSBPLAY_LOOPING | DSBPLAY_SYNCHPLAYBACK);
// Now, start them both playing in synch
hr = pDSound->SynchPlayback();

The SYNCHPLAYBACK state can be removed in a number of ways other than calling SynchPlayback. If the buffer or stream is paused with the standard PAUSE state, the SYNCHPLAYBACK state will be removed. The voice will not start when SynchPlayback is called. Instead, Pause must be called with the RESUME value. Calling Pause with the RESUME value will cause the SYNCHPLAYBACK and/or PAUSE states to be cleared and the voice will immediately start playback. Stopping a buffer or stream (including releasing the object and calling Flush on a stream) will also clear the SYNCHPLAYBACK state.

Requirements

Header: Declared in DSound.h.

Import Library: Use DSound.lib.
